summary: Stancil argues that DeepSeek shattered the assumed economics and trajectory of frontier LLMs, proving that powerful models can appear cheaply and unexpectedly, undermining the moat of big labs. This shift changes AI from a predictable, centralized race into a chaotic landscape of many specialized, uneven models with unpredictable societal impacts.
money_quotes: Every LLM vendor is eighteen months from dead.
We are no longer living in a world where a few superpowers might build Skynet, but one in which Skynet could be anywhere.
This is not a new world; it is a streamlined one.
AI cracks several more—that expertise takes time to accumulate; that reasoning has some foundational legitimacy; that creativity does not scale—and now, anyone can do anything, almost immediately.
key_insight: DeepSeek marks a discontinuity that democratizes frontier-level AI, making model proliferation and downstream societal disruption far more likely than the industry’s neat, centralized roadmap suggested.
